steps.title: Substitute y = 0 into the second equation
steps.content:
Since the first equation gives $y = 0$, substitute it into the second equation:
$$0 = 8(x^2 - 36)$$
Divide both sides by 8:
$$x^2 - 36 = 0$$

steps.title: Solve for x and match to the choices
steps.content:
Rearrange to $x^2 = 36$. Taking square roots: $x = 6$ or $x = -6$.
When $y = 0$, the ordered pairs are $(6, 0)$ and $(-6, 0)$. Only $(6, 0)$ appears in the choices. That matches choice C — C is correct.
